name: SPARC ADI — Application Data Integrity
summary: Direct conceptual ancestor of ARM MTE: 4-bit tag per 64-byte granule encoded in SPARC pointer top bits; hardware checks on load/store. Shipped on SPARC M7 (2015) through M8/T8; Solaris adiheap/adistack/libadimalloc/KADI. Effectively legacy as Oracle has wound down SPARC; the idea won via MTE.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**SPARC ADI (Application Data Integrity)** is the direct conceptual ancestor of ARM MTE: 4-bit tag per 64-byte granule encoded in SPARC pointer top bits; hardware checks on load/store. Shipped on SPARC M7 (2015) through M8/T8; Solaris `adiheap`, `adistack`, `libadimalloc`, KADI for kernel.

ADI was effectively the prototype hardware demonstration that per-allocation memory tagging works in production silicon — at a coarser granule (64-byte vs MTE's 16-byte) but with the same fundamental mechanism. Effectively legacy as Oracle has wound down SPARC; the *idea* won via MTE.
